source.title: How To Setup and Defend Coastline - Rainbow Six Siege
source.notes: Auto-generated YouTube captions with frequent ASR errors, especially on operator names and jargon. Two operator names required contextual reconstruction rather than a literal transcript match: (1) captions render 'Z me'/'zombie'/'keyo barrier'/'keeper barrier'/'aarm me' — from context (a claw-shaped barrier placed behind the bomb that blocks a repel window sightline and can be shot/disarmed) this is almost certainly Kaid's Electroclaw, reconstructed with reasonable confidence, not certainty. (2) captions render 'leion'/'legion' placing 'goo mines' to roam — 'Legion' isn't a real operator, and the Gu mine is literally Lesion's signature gadget, so this is treated as Lesion. A separate phrase, 'cap cans work really well on this door', could not be tied to any real defender gadget or operator name with confidence and is presented generically/unattributed rather than guessed. Room/callout names are the creator's informal terms and have not been reconciled against an official wiki. Rewrite pass: could not obtain reliable top-down blueprint grounding for Coastline (Ubisoft's blueprint download link 404s, r6maps.com requires JS rendering not available to this tool, and Liquipedia/Fandom/siege.gg/r6coaching pages returned no usable spatial/orientation detail) — per spec rule 8, no compass directions have been added anywhere in this file; all locations remain room/feature names only.

reinforcements.location: Hookah Door, Right Side
reinforcements.note: Reinforcement 1 of the standard 10 used on this site.

reinforcements.location: Hookah Door, Left Side
reinforcements.note: Reinforcement 2.

reinforcements.location: Triple Wall on Hookah, Left Side (next to Billiards)
reinforcements.note: Reinforcement 3.

reinforcements.location: Middle Wall Between Billiards and Hookah
reinforcements.note: Reinforcement 4.

reinforcements.location: Back of Pink Bar, All Four Walls
reinforcements.note: Reinforcements 5 through 8.

reinforcements.location: VIP Door Wall, VIP Wall, and Shotgun-Hole Wall
reinforcements.note: Option A for the last two reinforcements, if playing VIP. Lets Mute mute both VIP doors and this wall, deny drones, and rotate. Barricade the VIP exit door at round start — a roof camper commonly holds it. Consider a punch hole on the left side to shoot attackers off the wall when Ace Selmas it.

reinforcements.location: Alternate Wall Pair (if not playing VIP)
reinforcements.note: Option B for the last two reinforcements — reinforce this pair instead of the VIP-side walls.

reinforcements.location: Wall Near 90/VIP Exit
reinforcements.note: Play tight to this wall for cover — without it, someone from 90 or exiting VIP can wall-bang you through it. Still holds the corridor, the door, and 90 from behind it.

rotates.note: First of two rotates needed on this site. Make it a vault rotate.

gadgetPlacements.operator: Kaid (Electroclaw) — source audio rendered the name as 'Z me'/'zombie'; reconstructed from context, not a literal transcript match
gadgetPlacements.note: Place one Electroclaw barrier behind the bomb to block the Hookah window repel sightline.

gadgetPlacements.location: Towards Aqua, Over the Pool Table
gadgetPlacements.operator: Kaid (Electroclaw) — same naming caveat as above
gadgetPlacements.note: Angle over the pool table. With both barriers, fight Hookah window, fight/hold Hookah door, and rotate between rooms without exposure to Aqua.

gadgetPlacements.location: Middle Gap Between the Two Barriers
gadgetPlacements.operator: Kaid (Electroclaw) — same naming caveat as above
gadgetPlacements.note: Optional third barrier to fully block the line. Blocks your own fight-back onto Hookah window from that spot. Drop it to buy time to move through if held from the window — barriers are shootable.

operatorRecommendations.note: Mutes both VIP doors/jammers and the shotgun-hole wall to deny drones and seal the room.
Runs the Electroclaw barrier setup behind the bomb and toward Aqua. Name reconstructed from garbled audio — treat with some caution.

generalTips.note: 'Cap-cans' reportedly work well on the Hookah door for stopping a push through it — source audio unclear on exact gadget/operator, treat as unconfirmed.
Hold below around Sunrise/Kitchen/Office — uncontested access there gives uncontested access to Hookah door too. Play a shallow roam: present but able to respond.
This site gives up a free C4 onto Hookah door or into Aqua — use the default Aqua camera to time a drop-cam-then-C4 as attackers walk through the door.
A direct hit through the door straight to plant is possible on both Hookah and Aqua — hold map control even while roaming.

reinforcements.location: Service Wall to Kitchen
reinforcements.note: Reinforcement 1 of the 8 used downstairs at this site.

reinforcements.location: Kitchen Walls (both)
reinforcements.note: Reinforcements 2 and 3.

reinforcements.location: Barrels Walls (both)
reinforcements.note: Reinforcements 4 and 5.

reinforcements.location: Middle Wall Between Penthouse and VIP (Upstairs)
reinforcements.note: Reinforcement 6. Hold above this site — almost the entire floor above Kitchen is soft, so anyone controlling this room can shoot through the floor and deny re-entry.

reinforcements.location: Walls Outside VIP, Upstairs (both)
reinforcements.note: Reinforcements 7 and 8.

headHoles.note: One of two key lines of sight when holding the room above the site.

headHoles.location: Kitchen Door, From Upstairs Through the Floor
headHoles.note: Second key line of sight upstairs — shoots through the floor.

headHoles.location: Wall Watching Aqua/Hatch Drop, Feet Holes
headHoles.note: Made across the full wall so a defender in VIP can see feet through the hatch-drop area. Aim for the head once feet are seen.
Strong, learnable angle with any shotgun-capable loadout. Gu mines bounced off the desk toward it still trigger on contact even resting on the desk.

headHoles.location: Downward Angle Onto Back Barrels / Plant Corner
headHoles.note: Watch and punish a plant going down at the back of Barrels from above.

headHoles.location: Corridor Wall, Feet Holes (also angles onto Hall of Fame door)
headHoles.note: Used when playing the corridor rotate near the site.
Stops anyone planting in that pocket — otherwise near-uncontested via the door or rim rotate.

headHoles.location: Corridor Near Blue Door, Feet Holes
headHoles.note: Hard to hit with a shorty shotgun. Stops pushes through the corridor, holds Blue door, and eases retaking Kitchen without going through the main door.

rotates.note: Use the wardrobe as partial cover; also gives a small angle to hold the window.
Gives cover to fight Service door or fall back behind the reinforcement. Moves between the two halves of the site without going through the main door.

Left soft as a vault rotate rather than fully reinforced — gives another escape/hold angle (front door, a bit of Security/Courtyard) and eases retaking, at the cost of attackers reaching site faster if map control is lost early. Situational depending on round confidence.

hatches.note: Always leave open for a quick escape down into Bathroom if needed.

gadgetPlacements.note: Source audio rendered the name as 'leion'/'legion' — resolved to Lesion from the Gu mine gadget described. Roam here dropping Gu mines on the doorways.

gadgetPlacements.location: Kitchen Door and Kitchen Window, Downstairs
gadgetPlacements.operator: Lesion — same naming caveat as above
gadgetPlacements.note: A couple of Gu mines placed downstairs on top of the upstairs set.

operatorRecommendations.note: Holds the critical room above Kitchen with Gu mines on the doorways and downstairs approaches. Get denial on the floor-facing wall — losing it makes the site very hard to hold. Name reconstructed from garbled 'leion'/'legion' audio via the gadget described.

generalTips.note: Shoot the radio at the start.
Watch the drone hole near spawn — a free early drone kill is available almost every round in the first 15 seconds if ADS'd.
Two defenders needed on this site: one covering Kitchen, one covering Service.
Leaving the front-door-area wall soft also allows an impact grenade through it later in the round.
Losing map control early with the front-door rotate open lets attackers reach site faster — but it eases retaking when it works. Judgment call round to round.
Holding upstairs is the single biggest factor in winning this site.

reinforcements.location: Hookah-Adjacent Walls, Left Side (Upstairs)
reinforcements.note: First of 5 reinforcements used on this site.

reinforcements.location: Wall Between Sunrise and Blue Bar, Left Side (viewed from Sunrise)
reinforcements.note: Reinforcement.

reinforcements.location: Wall Between Hookah and Billiards (Upstairs)
reinforcements.note: One reinforcement placed upstairs.

reinforcements.location: Back of Blue Bar, Both Walls (behind attackers' hard-breach point)
reinforcements.note: Forces attackers to hard-breach rather than soft-breach straight in. Also lets defenders retake through the soft wall or drop the hatch to get behind Blue Bar.

reinforcements.location: Wall Stopping the Soft-Breach Route Into the Plant Corner
reinforcements.note: Left unreinforced, attackers can soft-breach straight through and vault the sofa into the tucked-away corner plant spot.

headHoles.location: Soft Walls Near the Jump-In Window, Feet Holes
headHoles.note: See feet before the attacker's head clears the doorway — stops people planting behind Blue Bar.

rotates.note: Mainly denies rather than fully holds this area.
Moves between halves without the main door — same purpose as the Kitchen site rotate.

hatches.note: Optional open. Gives a kill angle down onto the door into Sunrise for people running through to plant in that corner — defend upstairs hard if opened, since it's exposed.
Drop it to get a retaking defender behind Blue Bar or back through Security if attackers are dug in behind their reinforcement.

gadgetPlacements.location: Hidden Spots Away From the Jump-In Window
gadgetPlacements.note: Prox mines here trigger a warning the moment attackers jump in, without being shootable through the window first. Don't place directly above the door where visible from outside.
Almost the entire area is soft except one small bit near Hall of Fame — good visibility once attackers commit to the site.

operatorRecommendations.note: Plays the mostly-soft area near this site thanks to the amount of soft wall/floor to see through.

generalTips.note: Barely play inside Blue Bar itself — once the window is open you're effectively trapped there. This site is almost entirely about the retake, not the initial hold.
Attackers commonly hard-breach the back wall and plant tucked behind Blue Bar — not visible from the main walls there.
Don't give up Kitchen early — the anchor behind the bar already has plenty to watch without also worrying about being shot from Kitchen's direction.
Suggested spread: one or two upstairs (Hookah area, Billiards area), one on Sunrise-side, one on Office/Security/Courtyard.
The sofa vault into the tucked plant corner is inconsistent — don't rely on it working every time under time pressure.
Punishing to defend without a coordinated team.

reinforcements.location: Wall to Top of Main Stairs, Right Side
reinforcements.note: Reinforcement 1 of up to 10 used on this site.

reinforcements.location: Two Left-Hand Walls in Penthouse
reinforcements.note: Reinforced.

reinforcements.location: Penthouse Rotate Wall, Reinforced Side
reinforcements.note: Reinforce this side (or the opposite side, depending which side you want the rotate hole on) — creator prefers rotating on this side.

reinforcements.location: VIP Walls (both)
reinforcements.note: Reinforced.

reinforcements.location: Wall From VIP Toward Bay/Villa/Suite Area, Right Side
reinforcements.note: Reinforced — the third of the final three reinforcements.

reinforcements.location: Wall at Back of Theater (site-door side)
reinforcements.note: Debated reinforcement — without it, a defender at the back of Theater has no exit except the site door once Hall of Fame/bathroom are lost. A small rotate here instead gives a bit of a repel sightline to attackers but is generally worth it for mobility.

headHoles.location: Long Angle Into VIP From Behind the Reinforcement
headHoles.note: Weak angle for an SMG, strong with an ACOG. A mirror on this wall can also be used to see into VIP.

headHoles.location: Head Holes Near the Back-of-Theater Rotate
headHoles.note: Fight onto 90 for extra mobility out of the site without using the main doors.

rotates.location: Rotate Between the Two Sides, Near the Hatch
rotates.note: Add feet holes here too to shoot attackers dropping the hatch — doesn't stop the drop, but punishes it.

rotates.location: Small Rotate at Back of Theater (site-door side)
rotates.note: Keep it small to limit the repel sightline it gives attackers. If someone camps the repel spot, hold off using the rotate until they leave. Preferred over the opposite side because losing VIP plus this side's rotate lets attackers tuck into an undeniable corner plant.

hatches.location: Hatch Near the VIP Long-Angle Reinforcement
hatches.note: Open as an escape route to drop below if pushed.

gadgetPlacements.location: Line Across the Top, Angled at the Default Plant Corner
gadgetPlacements.note: A C4 or prox mine thrown from here lands on/near the default plant spot in the corner. A smoke needs to be lobbed off the ceiling to land correctly in the same spot.

gadgetPlacements.location: Feet Holes Watching the Walk-Down From the Corridor
gadgetPlacements.note: Catches attackers walking down before they know a defender is there. A prone attacker can still get an angle from the corridor — expect to cover that too.

operatorRecommendations.operator: Ela, Lesion, or another trap-style defender (e.g. Kapkan, Melusi)
operatorRecommendations.note: Covers both downstairs side approaches; a trap operator is preferred for up to three retreat routes back toward Main Lobby if map control is lost. Source audio named a defender phonetically similar to 'a fener', which doesn't match any real operator on this side — treat as unconfirmed; Ela/Lesion are plausible trap-operator fits, not a confirmed transcript match.
Usable, but a well-known enough angle that good attacking teams tend to expect it.
Almost all of Penthouse is soft except the small area near Hall of Fame.

generalTips.note: Losing VIP control combined with the rotate on the wrong side lets attackers tuck into an uncontestable corner plant.
In a disadvantage situation (1v2/2v1), use the small back-of-Theater rotate to reposition without attackers knowing, instead of defaulting to the site door.
